user_query:
Set up a small event-triggered automation in this workspace that generates structured Markdown meeting notes when the agenda changes, pulls in a short "Markdown tip" from an official source, and records reproducible metadata.

Inputs provided:
- input/agenda.md (draft agenda with a date in the first heading and bullet items)
- input/config.yaml (team members and keyword-to-owner preferences)

Requirements:
1) Event trigger: Implement a one-shot script (you choose the language) that only performs work when input/agenda.md has changed since the last successful run. Use a content hash file at out/state/agenda.sha256 to detect changes. If no changes are detected, the script should exit without rewriting outputs but append a brief entry to out/logs/run.log.

2) Inspect configuration: Read and use input/config.yaml to assign action items. For each agenda bullet in input/agenda.md, find the first matching keyword (case-insensitive) in any team member’s owns_keywords list and assign the item to that member. If no keyword matches, assign the fallback_owner from the config. Use only the provided config to drive these assignments.

3) Download remote resource: Fetch the raw Markdown content of the README.md from the official GitHub repository commonmark/commonmark-spec maintained by the commonmark organization (identify the file by organization "commonmark", repository "commonmark-spec", path "README.md"). Save the fetched content to out/downloads/commonmark_README.md. Also write out/metadata/commonmark_source.json with fields: { "source_id": "github:commonmark/commonmark-spec:README.md", "fetched_at": ISO-8601 timestamp, "sha256": hex digest of the file, "bytes": integer size }.

4) Prepare meeting notes: Parse the meeting date from the first heading line of input/agenda.md (format: "# Agenda - YYYY-MM-DD"). Generate a Markdown file at out/notes/YYYY-MM-DD-meeting.md (use the parsed date) with these sections in order:
   - # Meeting Notes (include the parsed date on this line)
   - ## Agenda (echo each original bullet item verbatim)
   - ## Action Items (one bullet per agenda item, formatted as "Name: Follow up on — <original item>")
   - ## Markdown Tip (include the first Markdown heading text found in the downloaded README, and if a fenced code block exists in that file include the first one; otherwise include the first 120 characters of the README content)

5) Logging and idempotence: Append a concise run summary to out/logs/run.log on every invocation. On a run that performs work, update out/state/agenda.sha256 to the new hash so that a subsequent run with no changes does nothing. Do not hardcode any remote content in the repo; it must be fetched at runtime.

Deliverables to create under the workspace upon a triggering change:
- out/notes/YYYY-MM-DD-meeting.md (as specified)
- out/downloads/commonmark_README.md (downloaded content)
- out/metadata/commonmark_source.json (with the required fields)
- out/state/agenda.sha256 (content hash of input/agenda.md)
- out/logs/run.log (appended each run)

Please keep the implementation self-contained and runnable locally. Make the Internet fetch an essential step (the notes must incorporate the downloaded content), and make all paths above exact. You may choose any language or tools, but the outputs and their structure must match these requirements. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
